tasks.prompt: Design a 32-bit Program Counter (PC) module for a single-cycle RISC-V CPU. The PC must increment by 4 on every clock cycle (since instructions are 32-bit/4-byte aligned). Support branch and jump control signals: when branch_taken or jump signals are asserted, load the target address (from ALU output or register+immediate); otherwise, increment PC by 4. Initialize PC to 0x00000000 on reset. Output the current PC value for instruction fetch and provide PC+4 for link register writes (jalr instruction). Implement synchronous reset and clock-gated updates.

Design an Immediate Generator (ImmGen) module that decodes and sign-extends immediate values from RISC-V instructions. Support all required immediate formats: I-type (12-bit sign-extended for addi, lw, jalr), S-type (12-bit sign-extended for sw), B-type (13-bit sign-extended for beq), and U-type (20-bit for lui). The module receives a 32-bit instruction and a 3-bit imm_sel control signal (I, S, B, U, or X for don't-care). Output a 32-bit sign-extended immediate value. Ensure correct bit extraction and sign extension per RISC-V specification.

Design an ALU Decoder module that maps main decoder control signals and instruction fields to ALU control signals. Inputs: 2-bit alu_op (from main decoder), 3-bit funct3, 7-bit funct7 from instruction. Output: 3-bit alu_control. Implement the exact truth table: alu_op=00 (Load/Store) → ADD (000); alu_op=01 (Branch) → SUB (001); alu_op=10 (R-type) → decode funct3/funct7 to ADD/SUB/AND/OR. For funct3=000: funct7[5]=0 → ADD (000), funct7[5]=1 → SUB (001). For funct3=111 → AND (010). For funct3=110 → OR (011). Default to ADD for undefined combinations.

Design a 32-bit ALU (Arithmetic Logic Unit) for RISC-V. Inputs: two 32-bit operands (A, B), 3-bit alu_control signal. Outputs: 32-bit result, zero flag. Implement the following operations based on alu_control: 000=ADD (A+B), 001=SUB (A-B), 010=AND (A&B), 011=OR (A|B). The zero flag is asserted when result==0 (used for branch conditions). Ensure correct overflow/underflow handling for signed arithmetic. This module is already available in the IP inventory; reuse it directly.

Design a 32-entry Register File (x0-x31) for RISC-V. Inputs: two 5-bit read addresses (rs1, rs2), one 5-bit write address (rd), 32-bit write data (wd), write enable (we), clock, reset. Outputs: two 32-bit read data ports (rd1, rd2). CRITICAL HARDWARE RULE: Register x0 (R[0]) is hardwired to 0. Any write to x0 must be ignored (we is masked for rd==0). Any read from x0 must always return 32'b0. Implement synchronous writes on clock edge; asynchronous reads. Initialize all registers to 0 on reset.

Design the Main Decoder (Control Unit) for a single-cycle RISC-V CPU. Input: 7-bit opcode from instruction[6:0]. Outputs: reg_write, imm_sel (3-bit: I/S/B/U/X), alu_src (0=register, 1=immediate), alu_op (2-bit), mem_write, mem_read, branch, jump. Implement the exact truth table from the knowledge base: add (0110011) → reg_write=1, imm_sel=X, alu_src=0, alu_op=10, others=0; addi (0010011) → reg_write=1, imm_sel=I, alu_src=1, alu_op=00, others=0; lw (0000011) → reg_write=1, imm_sel=I, alu_src=1, alu_op=00, mem_read=1, others=0; sw (0100011) → reg_write=0, imm_sel=S, alu_src=1, alu_op=00, mem_write=1, others=0; beq (1100011) → reg_write=0, imm_sel=B, alu_src=0, alu_op=01, branch=1, others=0; jalr (1100111) → reg_write=1, imm_sel=I, alu_src=1, alu_op=00, jump=1, others=0. Default all outputs to 0 for undefined opcodes.

Design the top-level single-cycle RISC-V CPU core implementing the OSOC F6 minirv subset. Integrate the following sub-modules: Program Counter, Main Decoder, ALU Decoder, ALU, Register File, and Immediate Generator. Datapath: Fetch instruction from imem_rdata (external instruction memory). Decode opcode to control signals via Main Decoder. Read two registers (rs1, rs2) from Register File. Select second ALU operand (register or immediate via alu_src). Decode ALU operation via ALU Decoder. Execute ALU operation. For loads, read from external data memory (dmem_rdata). For stores, write to external data memory (dmem_wdata, dmem_we). Write result to Register File (rd). Update PC: increment by 4 normally, or jump to target address (register+immediate for jalr, or PC+immediate for beq if zero flag set). Expose external memory interfaces: imem_addr (PC output), imem_rdata (32-bit instruction input), dmem_addr (ALU result), dmem_rdata (32-bit data input), dmem_wdata (register data output), dmem_we (write enable). Implement clock and reset. This is the top-level integration; use a dummy oracle for verification.
